title: Anteriorchis fragrans
taxonomy: Anteriorchis fragrans is classified as a genus and species within the orchid family (Orchidaceae). It belongs to the subfamily Orchidoideae. Further classification places it in the tribe Orchideae. Anteriorchis fragrans is native to Western Australia. Its classification as a species is a part of the larger orchid classification system.

intro: Anteriorchis fragrans, also known as the sweet autumn clematis or fringed clematis, is a perennial flowering plant that produces fragrant, bell-shaped flowers in late summer and early fall. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can aid in wound healing and reduce fever. The bioactive constituents of Anteriorchis fragrans include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat a range of ailments, from respiratory issues to digestive problems.
